questionHtml: The pH of an aqueous solution of benzoic acid at 298 K is 2.95. Determine the concentration of hydroxide ions in the solution, using section 2 of the data booklet.
markschemeHtml:
ALTERNATIVE 1:
[H+] «= 10−2.95» = 1.122 × 10−3 «mol dm−3»  [✔]

«[OH−] =  1.00 × 10 − 14  mo l 2  d m − 6 1.22 × 10 − 3  mol d m − 3 =» 8.91 × 10−12 «mol dm−3»  [✔]

 

ALTERNATIVE 2:
pOH = «14 − 2.95 =» 11.05  [✔]

«[OH−] = 10−11.05 =» 8.91 × 10−12 «moldm−3»  [✔]

 

Note: Award [2] for correct final answer.

Accept other methods.

meta.title: The pH of an aqueous solution of benzoic acid at 298 K is 2.95. Determine the concentration of hydroxide ions in the solution, using section 2 of the data booklet.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.1.5—The ion product constant of water, Kw, shows an inverse relationship between [H+] and [OH–]. Kw = [H+] [OH–]. Recognize solutions as acidic, neutral and basic from the relative values of [H+] and [OH–].
Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure
Structure 2.2.12—Benzene, C6H6, is an important example of a molecule that has resonance. Discuss the structure of benzene from physical and chemical evidence.
